name: Vegetable Terrine
recipeIngredient: 20 ounces frozen spinach (2 packs), squeezed dry
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F.
In a blender or food processor, combine eggs, milk, flour, and salt. Process until smooth. Reserve.
Melt butter in a medium skillet over moderate heat. Add onion and cook, stirring, until softened. Remove from heat and stir in spinach and about one-third of the reserved batter. Reserve.
Bring a medium pot of water to a boil and cook peeled carrots for about 6 minutes, depending on thickness, until tender but still crisp. With a slotted spoon, transfer to a plate and allow to cool.
Add cauliflower florets to the boiling water and cook for about 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon and allow to cool slightly.
Butter a 2-quart loaf pan, then butter a piece of aluminum foil large enough to line it with a slight overhang. Line the pan with the foil, fitting it into the corners and allowing it to hang over the edges.
Place half the cauliflower in the base of the pan and pour over half of the remaining batter. Next, spread half of the spinach mixture over the cauliflower in an even layer. Lay the carrots lengthwise next to each other in the pan. Spread the remaining spinach over the carrots, then the remaining cauliflower, and press gently.
Pour over the remaining batter. Butter a piece of aluminum foil large enough to cover the pan. Place over the vegetables, seal with the overhanging foil, and gently press down the foil.
Place pan in a larger roasting pan and pour water into the larger pan so that it reaches about halfway up the sides of the loaf pan. Bake for 1 hour.
Remove from oven and remove loaf pan from water. Allow to cool for at least an hour. Remove foil from top and invert on a plate. Carefully peel off the foil and cut across into 1-inch slices.
